organism: Photorhabdus laumondii subsp. laumondii (strain DSM 15139 / CIP 105565 / TT01) (Photorhabdus luminescens subsp. laumondii)

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ABC ATPase that energises export and forms the IM channel
machinery_genes.note: Rodou et al. 2010 Table 1 lists ABC 4 = PrtB (NP_928002, genome 754,786-756,516) as the ABC/ATP-binding component of one of the six P. luminescens TT01 T1SS, 70.4% identity to LipB. This is the prtB of the prtA-inh-prtBCD operon (Bowen et al. 2003). Maps to plu0657 in the prtA(plu0655)-inh(plu0656)-prtBCD operon order; locus tag inferred from operon structure, not asserted.

machinery_genes.role: periplasm-spanning IM-anchored adaptor bridging the ABC transporter to the OMP
machinery_genes.note: Rodou et al. 2010 Table 1 lists MFP 4 = PrtC (NP_928003, genome 756,577-757,911) as the membrane fusion protein of this T1SS, 62.7% identity to LipC. The prtC of the prtA-inh-prtBCD operon (Bowen et al. 2003). Maps to plu0658 by operon order; locus tag inferred, not asserted.

machinery_genes.role: TolC-family outer-membrane channel forming the exit duct
machinery_genes.quote: The prtA gene resides in a locus encoding both the protease ABC transporter (prtBCD) and an intervening ORF encoding a protease inhibitor (inh).
machinery_genes.note: PrtD is the D of the prtBCD apparatus named by Bowen et al. 2003. Its OMP/TolC role is corroborated by Rodou et al. 2010 Table 1, which lists TolC 2 = NP_928004 (genome 757,911-759,287, immediately 3' of PrtC, 50.4% identity to LipD) as the 'Outer membrane protein' of this system. NP_928004 is the prtD/lipD-like outer-membrane channel of the prtA-inh-prtBCD operon. This is the sys_instance_label gene plu0659 (terminal gene of the operon, immediately downstream of the effector plu0655); locus mapping from operon order, not asserted from the GenBank.

notes: System = the PrtA T1SS of Photorhabdus laumondii TT01. PrtA (the secreted RTX-like alkaline zinc metalloprotease, the curated effector plu0655) is exported by a classical three-component Type I secretion system encoded in the same locus: the prtA-inh-prtBCD operon. Bowen et al. 2003 (Microbiology) genetically and biochemically characterized PrtA and named prtBCD as the protease ABC transporter; they showed prtBCD encodes a functional T1SS that secretes PrtA from recombinant cells. The three apparatus genes map to the three canonical T1SS families: PrtB = ABC ATPase (IM), PrtC = membrane fusion protein (MFP, HlyD-like), PrtD = TolC-family outer-membrane channel. Per-component naming and family assignment for PrtB and PrtC come verbatim from Rodou et al. 2010 Table 1 (their 'System 4': ABC 4 = PrtB NP_928002, MFP 4 = PrtC NP_928003, TolC 2 = NP_928004 LipD-like OMP), which catalogued the six TT01 T1SS by BLAST against known T1SS components (lipase LipBCD of Serratia/Pseudomonas). The operon gene order (prtA, inh, prtB, prtC, prtD) is consistent with the instance labelling: effector plu0655 = prtA, apparatus label plu0659 = prtD (terminal OMP). Locus-tag-to-gene mapping (plu0657/0658/0659 = prtB/C/D) is given only in notes and inferred from operon structure; it was NOT mined from the RefSeq GenBank. Deliberately excluded as non-machinery: PrtA itself (the secreted substrate/effector), and Inh (the periplasmic Sec-translocated protease inhibitor, an accessory/maturation protein, not a transport-apparatus component). All quotes verbatim from the cited abstract (Bowen 2003) and PMC full-text Table 1 (Rodou 2010). No standalone TolC shared from elsewhere is invoked: PrtD/LipD-like NP_928004 is the operon-internal OMP. Status COMPLETE: all three core T1SS components (ABC, MFP, OMP) documented with naming quotes.
